scenarios.query:
Review this changed SKILL.md frontmatter.
```yaml
---
name: helper
description: I help with reviewing things.
---
```
expected_behavior: Detects the artifact is a SKILL.md and reviews it against the skill-authoring rubric, not as source code

Flags the description as a non-trigger: first-person, vague, and naming no concrete situations/keywords or skip clause — so the model cannot tell when to activate the skill; recommends a specific third-person description with triggers
Flags that `name: helper` is not a gerund, lowercase-hyphen name the standard expects

scenarios.query: Review this SKILL.md (the body is ~700 lines: it inlines the full checklist, every tool rule, and all references inline; the skill has no reference/ files at all).
expected_behavior: Flags a progressive-disclosure violation: the body far exceeds the ~500-line lean-entry-point target and inlines detail that belongs in bundled, one-level-deep reference files loaded on demand
Explains the cost: an inlined mega-body taxes every session's context, not just the reviewing one
Recommends moving the heuristics/tool-rules/references into reference/*.md and keeping the body to when-to-use plus a short checklist

scenarios.query: Review this SKILL.md: gerund `name`, a specific third-person `description` (~300 chars) with explicit triggers and a 'skip when' clause, a ~90-line body, bundled reference/heuristics.md and examples.md, three eval scenarios, and no time-sensitive wording.
expected_behavior: Recognizes the artifact already meets the authoring standard on every rubric line (frontmatter, progressive disclosure, triggers, examples, evals)
Does not invent a deviation on a well-formed artifact

scenarios.query: Review this change: a diff that only modifies src/payments.ts — there is no SKILL.md or other authored artifact anywhere in the change.
expected_behavior: Recognizes this is presence-activated and none of its artifacts are present, so there is nothing for it to review
Does not review the .ts source against an artifact rubric — ordinary source code is the other lenses' job
